baseline_guidance: Compare responses with and without this wrapper to confirm the agent chooses the CLI, starts read-only, and preserves repair consent.

evals.prompt: Audit my local Agent Skills for quality and security issues and summarize what needs repair.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs or recommends a read-only skills-doctor JSON scan first, treats scanner findings as canonical, and summarizes repair priorities from the report.
assertions: Response starts with a read-only scan using skills-doctor --json.
Response treats scan findings as the source of truth for repair scope.

evals.prompt: Give me machine-readable Skills Doctor findings that another tool can consume.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses skills-doctor --json --json-compact or an equivalent JSON mode and keeps human logs out of the machine-readable output.
assertions: Response chooses skills-doctor --json --json-compact for embedded automation.
Response preserves JSON output for downstream tooling.

evals.prompt: Launch the Skills Doctor repair handoff for these skill findings.
evals.expected_output: The agent confirms explicit user consent before launching the interactive skills-doctor repair flow and prefers a read-only audit first if one has not run.
assertions: Response asks for explicit consent before interactive repair handoff.
Response does not launch repair before a read-only scan or approval.
